channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Legal Requirements for Claiming Maintenance from Siblings in South Africa
item.description: In South Africa, individuals who are struggling financially may be entitled to claim maintenance from their siblings under certain legal conditions. This provision, which has existed since 1938 in the...

item.title: The Enduring Partnership of Laurie and Elizabeth Baker in Building Community and Architecture
item.description: Laurie Baker, a British-born architect, and Elizabeth Baker, a Malayali physician, formed a unique partnership that significantly impacted Kerala's architecture and community life. Laurie arrived in I...

item.title: SHA Digital System Outage Disrupts Pre-Authorisation for Healthcare Nationwide
item.description: On 1st March 2026, the Social Health Authority (SHA) in Kenya experienced a major system failure affecting its digital health platform. This outage has disrupted pre-authorisation services across all ...

item.title: US and Niger Agree on Five-Year Health Cooperation Plan
item.description: On Thursday, February 26, 2026, the United States and Niger waka enter a five-year health cooperation Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) worth almost $178 million. Dis agreement dey under the Trump Adm...

item.title: Uni Challenge wins friendly football match against Guardian of the Peak
item.description: DAR ES SALAAM – Just days after celebrating the conquest of Mount Kilimanjaro, the Uni Challenge football team proved their strength again by defeating the Guardian of the Peak team 6-4 in a penalty s...

item.title: Algeria and Niger Move to Restore Diplomatic Ties Amid Sahel Security and Energy Interests
item.description: After almost one year of diplomatic wahala, Niger and Algeria don begin test one careful reconciliation wey fit change the political and security balance for the Sahel region. President Abdourahamane ...

item.title: Pakistani TV Channels and Websites Face Coordinated Cyberattacks; CERT Launches Probe
item.description: On Sunday night, multiple Pakistani television channels, websites, and mobile applications faced coordinated cyberattacks, prompting the National Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT) to start an of...

item.title: Council rules against Welsh-only housing condition in Trefor development
item.description: Gwynedd Council has approved a development of 15 affordable homes in the seaside village of Trefor without including a stipulation that new properties be reserved exclusively for Welsh speakers. The p...

item.title: Hillary Clinton expresses anger over leaked deposition photo by Rep. Boebert
item.description: During a deposition, former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton became visibly upset when Republican Representative Lauren Boebert of Colorado leaked a photo from the proceedings. The release of the ph...

item.title: Jontay Porter to resume professional basketball in the USBL after NBA ban
item.description: Jontay Porter, former centre for the Toronto Raptors, has signed with the Seattle Superhawks of the independent United States Basketball League (USBL), signalling his return to professional basketball...

item.title: Six people killed and others trapped after structural failure at Amethyst Business Park, Johannesburg
item.description: On Monday, a tragic incident occurred in Ormonde, south of Johannesburg, when a concrete slab on the first floor of a two-storey building at Amethyst Business Park collapsed. The structural failure re...

item.title: Tanzania Launches National Inquiry on Election Violence, Receives UN Recognition
item.description: The United Nations Human Rights Council has praised Tanzania for taking quick and responsible steps after the violent incidents that followed the October 2025 General Election. Speaking at the 61st se...

item.title: Supreme Court Rules Employers Must Bear Penalty for Delayed Compensation Payments
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has clarified that employers are personally liable to pay penalties for delays in compensation under the Employees' Compensation Act, even if the main compensation amount is...

item.title: PCB imposes Rs5 million fines on players for underperforming in T20 World Cup
item.description: The Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) has decided to fine each member of the national T20 squad Rs5 million following their disappointing performance at the 2026 T20 World Cup, according to reports. These ...

item.description: The fourth and final day of the International Criminal Court (ICC) confirmation of charges hearing against former President Rodrigo Duterte took place on February 27, 2026, in The Hague. The hearing f...

item.description: In a heated session of the Lok Sabha, Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi cited excerpts from former Army Chief General MM Naravane’s unpublished memoir, claiming Chinese tanks had entered Indian territ...

item.title: Triple killer Valdo Calocane's history of mental health incidents examined at Nottingham Inquiry
item.description: The Nottingham Inquiry has revealed that triple killer Valdo Calocane entered a woman's flat just 11 minutes after being released from custody in 2020, leaving her seriously injured when she fell from...

item.title: Government to Pay Funeral Expenses for MP Ng'eno and Victims of Mosop Helicopter Crash
item.description: President William Ruto has announced that the government will fully cover the funeral expenses of Emurua Dikirr MP Johanna Ng'eno and the five other individuals who tragically died in the helicopter c...

item.title: Understanding Africa’s Public Finance Challenges and Paths to Reform
item.description: This article discusses the new book 'Governing Public Money' by Lyla Latif, a Kenyan public finance specialist. Latif draws from over ten years of experience across 32 African countries to analyse why...
